decisions.reasoning: Q1 FY2027 earnings reported May 20 after close: EPS $1.87 vs $1.78 est, revenue $81.6B (+85% YoY) vs $79.2B est, Q2 guidance $91B vs $86.84B est. CEO Huang: demand gone parabolic, agentic AI arrived. Position is +2.3% from entry. Despite fourth-straight post-earnings slide pattern and macro headwinds today (rising yields, oil, SPY slipping), thesis is intact and churn rule (position only 2 days old) applies. SMA signal remains long.

sources.headline: Nvidia earnings takeaways: Data center revenue nearly doubles, report is strong but stock slides

decisions.invalidation: Q2 revenue misses $91B guidance, data center demand softens, or US export restriction tightening on AI chips

decisions.reasoning: Churn rule applies (2-day-old position, no new catalyst today). Significant bear risks identified: OpenAI capped Microsoft revenue sharing at $38B and expanded partnerships to AWS (no longer Azure-exclusive); commercial bookings fell 46% on constant currency; capex blowout to $190B vs $150B guide; FCF -22.2% YoY. Stock sat between $405-$432 range; SMA cluster at ~$415 is near-term support. Risk of breakdown below $400 noted. Watching for new negative catalysts before considering exit.

decisions.invalidation: Breakdown below $405 support, or new negative news on OpenAI relationship or Azure cloud bookings — would exit

decisions.reasoning: Core broad market exposure. Up +0.9% from entry. SMA signal long (fast $739.78 > slow $719.98). Today market under mild pressure (yields up, oil up, Walmart weak guidance) but no material reason to exit core ETF anchor. Provides balance to concentrated tech positions.

sources.headline: Stock Market Today: Dow edges down on inflation fears
Stock Market Live May 21, 2026: S&P 500 (SPY) Slips as Oil Gushes Higher

decisions.invalidation: Broad market breakdown below 200-day SMA or systemic risk event

decisions.reasoning: Initiated new position at $59.94 (10 shares, ~6% of equity). Thesis: Iran/Strait of Hormuz war is the largest oil supply disruption since the 1970s energy crisis. Strait closure affects ~20% of global oil trade; Brent peaked $126/barrel, oil up 40% from pre-war levels. De-escalation talks in March produced no resolution. Today oil surged +$2.45 (macro headwind for market but tailwind for energy). XLE entered after pullback from $61.27 high (May 19) to $59.93 today on high volume, providing a better entry within the uptrend. XLE +21.6% YTD, MACD turned positive May 15, momentum positive. Provides sector diversification against our tech-heavy portfolio (NVDA, MSFT). Position sized conservatively at 6% of equity given geopolitical tail risk of rapid de-escalation.

sources.headline: 2026 Strait of Hormuz crisis — Largest oil supply disruption since 1970s
How long can we look through the Iran war commodity shock?
Stock Market Live May 21: S&P 500 slips as oil gushes higher

decisions.invalidation: Ceasefire or meaningful Hormuz reopening, or oil drops decisively below $90/barrel WTI — would trim or exit

summary: Held NVDA/MSFT/SPY (all 2-day-old positions); initiated XLE on Iran/Hormuz structural oil-supply-shock thesis as sector diversifier
open_theses: NVDA: Post-earnings fundamental inflection; data center revenue doubling, Q2 guide of $91B; riding AI infrastructure supercycle. Watch for analyst upgrades to flow through.
MSFT: Monitoring OpenAI partnership deterioration risk — cap at $38B revenue share and AWS migration are structural negatives. Hold while above $405 SMA support.
SPY: Core market anchor; hold unless broad market technicals break down.
XLE: Iran/Hormuz Strait closure is structural oil supply shock since Feb 2026; thesis intact while conflict continues and Hormuz remains restricted. Watching for ceasefire as invalidation.
